name: Strawberry Hot Springs
parking: Limited lot, arrive early on weekends
description: Five natural soaking pools with mountain views near Steamboat Springs. One of Colorado's most popular hot springs.
insider_tips: The uppermost pool is hottest. Weeknight visits are much less crowded.
nearby_gems: Fish Creek Falls (5 miles), Steamboat Ski Resort

name: Iron Mountain Hot Springs
description: 16 soaking pools along the Colorado River with views of Glenwood Canyon. Family-friendly with a freshwater pool.
insider_tips: Riverside pools fill first — grab one early. Combo tickets with Glenwood Caverns available.

name: Glenwood Hot Springs Pool
description: World's largest mineral hot springs pool. Two pools — large lap pool and hot therapy pool — plus waterslides and spa.

name: Wiesbaden Hot Springs
description: Historic hotel in Ouray with underground vapor cave, outdoor soaking pool, and private tubs. Adults only.
insider_tips: The underground vapor cave is a unique experience not found elsewhere in Colorado.
nearby_gems: Ouray Hot Springs Pool, Box Canyon Falls

name: Valley View Hot Springs
description: Peaceful clothing-optional hot springs run by Orient Land Trust. Multiple pools with stunning San Luis Valley views.
insider_tips: Book well in advance — fills up fast. Camping on site available.

name: Conundrum Hot Springs
description: Remote 17-mile round-trip hike to natural hot springs at 11,200 ft near Aspen. Worth every step.
insider_tips: Camp overnight to enjoy pools in morning solitude. Altitude sickness is a real risk.

name: Rainbow Hot Springs
description: Free backcountry hot springs on the West Fork of the San Juan River. 3.5-mile hike each way through beautiful canyon.
best_time: Spring and fall for lower crowds
insider_tips: River crossings can be high in spring snowmelt — check conditions. Multiple pools at different temperatures.

name: Cottonwood Hot Springs
description: Clothing-optional resort near Buena Vista with multiple natural mineral pools and lodging.
insider_tips: Multiple pools at different temperatures. Weekday afternoons are quiet.
nearby_gems: Mount Princeton Hot Springs (15 min), Arkansas River rafting

name: Penny Hot Springs
description: Free roadside hot spring on the Crystal River near Carbondale. Natural pools depend on river level — best in summer and fall.
alerts: High water can submerge pools in spring
insider_tips: Can get very crowded on summer weekends. Visit early morning or late evening.

name: South Canyon Hot Springs
parking: Small pullout off I-70 frontage road
description: Free natural hot springs near Glenwood Springs reached by a short hike along a creek. Wild feel close to town.
insider_tips: Easy to find once you know it — look for cars near milepost 113 on the frontage road.

name: Salida Hot Springs Aquatic Center
description: Indoor mineral hot springs pool in downtown Salida — the largest indoor hot springs pool in the US. Affordable and family friendly.
insider_tips: Private soaking tubs available by reservation. Great after a day of rafting the Arkansas.

name: Joyful Journey Hot Springs
description: Three outdoor mineral soaking pools in the San Luis Valley with 360-degree mountain views. Yoga and lodging available.
best_time: Morning for mountain views, night for stargazing
insider_tips: The coldest pool is perfect for contrast therapy. Night soaking under the stars is incredible.

name: Dunton Hot Springs
description: Ultra-luxury ghost town resort in the San Juan Mountains with private hot springs. Restored 1800s cabins. Exclusive and remote.
insider_tips: Overnight stays only — no day visits. One of the most remote and beautiful settings in Colorado.

name: Avalanche Ranch Hot Springs
description: Three outdoor mineral pools on a working ranch near Redstone with views of Avalanche Creek and Crystal River Valley.
insider_tips: Cabins and glamping available. Quieter and more relaxed than Glenwood.

name: Radium Hot Springs
description: Wild backcountry hot springs along the Colorado River near Kremmling. Popular with kayakers and river runners.
insider_tips: Accessible via raft or kayak during high water — one of the best river hot spring combos in the state.

name: Cement Creek Hot Springs
parking: Cement Creek TH near Crested Butte
description: Remote free hot springs near Crested Butte, requiring a moderate hike through alpine scenery.
insider_tips: Often missed by tourists — a local favorite near Crested Butte.

name: Chalk Creek Hot Springs (Hortense)
parking: Chalk Creek Rd pullout near Nathrop
description: Free natural hot springs near Mount Princeton. Very hot at source — mix with creek water in pools you build yourself.
alerts: Water at source can be scalding — always test before entering
insider_tips: Build your own pools by digging into the bank. Bring a shovel.

name: Schofield Hot Springs
description: Backcountry hot springs near Gothic in the Elk Mountains. Remote and spectacular alpine setting.
insider_tips: Road to Gothic requires 4WD. Combine with a visit to the Rocky Mountain Biological Laboratory.

name: Box Canyon Hot Springs
description: Rooftop hot tubs at Box Canyon Lodge in Ouray with stunning canyon views. Small and intimate.
insider_tips: Day passes available but book ahead — fills quickly.

name: Antero Hot Springs
description: Warm soaking pools on BLM land near South Park. More warm than hot, but free and scenic.

name: Routt Hot Springs
parking: Small pullout off CR 129 near Steamboat Lake
description: Remote natural hot springs north of Steamboat Springs. Simple pools with a wild, undeveloped feel.
insider_tips: Local secret north of Steamboat. Look for the pullout near mile marker 12 on CR 129.

name: Tenderfoot Hot Springs
description: Natural hot springs near Steamboat Lake in a remote, forested setting. Undeveloped and peaceful.
insider_tips: Combine with a visit to Steamboat Lake State Park.

name: Eldorado Springs Resort
description: Historic spring-fed swimming pool in Eldorado Canyon near Boulder. Cool mineral water — perfect for summer swimming.
insider_tips: More of a cool mineral pool than hot spring, but great for families near Boulder. Bring cash.

name: Fish Creek Hot Springs
parking: Trailhead off Forest Road near Yampa
description: Secluded backcountry hot springs in the Flat Tops Wilderness near Steamboat Springs. Rewarding hike through pristine wilderness.
insider_tips: One of the more remote springs in Colorado. Plan for a full day trip.

name: Paradise Hot Springs
description: Family-friendly hot springs resort near Pagosa Springs with multiple pools and camping.
insider_tips: Less crowded than The Springs Resort in Pagosa. Good value for families.

name: Poncha Hot Springs
description: Clothing-optional resort near Salida with indoor and outdoor soaking pools fed by natural mineral springs.
insider_tips: Recently renovated. One of the more affordable resort options in central Colorado.

name: Hartsel Hot Springs
description: Small soaking operation in South Park near fishing and wide-open plains. Simple and affordable.
alerts: Operating status changes frequently — verify before visiting
insider_tips: One of the most affordable soaking options on the Front Range side of the Rockies.

name: Rico Hot Springs
description: Wild, undeveloped hot springs along the Dolores River in a remote canyon near the old mining town of Rico.
insider_tips: Rarely crowded. Beautiful canyon setting along the Dolores River.

name: Mount Princeton Hot Springs
description: Popular resort near Buena Vista with multiple outdoor pools fed by naturally superheated springs. Creekside soaking pools are free to access from the road.
insider_tips: The free riverside pools along Chalk Creek Rd are just upstream — arrive early to snag a spot.

name: Idaho Springs Soda Springs
description: Historic mineral springs soaking facility just 45 minutes west of Denver off I-70. Easy mountain getaway.
insider_tips: One of the closest hot springs to Denver. Great for a quick after-work soak.
